name: BPC-157
mechanism_brief: Pentadecapeptide derived from gastric juice protein BPC. Modulates VEGF/NO axis, accelerates tendon, ligament and gut tissue regeneration.
biohacker_use: Subcutaneous 250-500mcg/day for tendon/ligament injury, gut healing, soft-tissue regeneration. Frequent stack with TB-500 in regenerative protocols.

name: TB-500
mechanism_brief: Active region of thymosin beta-4 sequestering G-actin and promoting cell migration, angiogenesis and tissue repair.
biohacker_use: 2-2.5mg subcutaneous twice weekly during acute injury, then taper. Synergistic with BPC-157 in regenerative stacks.

name: MOTS-c
mechanism_brief: Mitochondrial-derived peptide encoded within MT-RNR1. Activates AMPK, modulates folate-methionine metabolism and metabolic homeostasis.
biohacker_use: 5-10mg subcutaneous, often morning. Used in metabolic and longevity stacks for insulin sensitivity, exercise performance.

name: Humanin
mechanism_brief: Mitochondrial-derived peptide; binds BAX to inhibit apoptosis, neuroprotective, modulates IGF axis.
biohacker_use: Limited human dosing data. Investigated for neuroprotection and metabolic resilience. Often discussed alongside MOTS-c.

name: Semaglutide
mechanism_brief: Long-acting GLP-1 receptor agonist with substituted Aib at position 2 and a fatty-acid-modified Lys for albumin binding.
biohacker_use: Weekly subcutaneous 0.25-2.4mg for weight management and glycaemic control. Standard of care for obesity / T2D.

name: Tirzepatide
mechanism_brief: Dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonist with C20 fatty diacid modification for once-weekly pharmacokinetics.
biohacker_use: Weekly subcutaneous 2.5-15mg. Strong weight-loss profile alongside glycaemic control.

name: Retatrutide
mechanism_brief: Triple agonist (GLP-1 / GIP / glucagon). Investigational; phase 2 reported substantial weight loss.
biohacker_use: Investigational. Weekly subcutaneous in trials; not approved.

name: Ipamorelin
mechanism_brief: Selective ghrelin receptor agonist; pulse-releases growth hormone with minimal cortisol/prolactin elevation.
biohacker_use: 200-300mcg subcutaneous, 1-3x/day. Frequent stack with CJC-1295 for GH pulses targeted at recovery and body composition.

name: Sermorelin
mechanism_brief: Truncated active fragment of GHRH; stimulates pituitary GH release.
biohacker_use: 200-500mcg subcutaneous, evening. Used to support physiological GH pulse pattern.

name: Tesamorelin
mechanism_brief: Stabilised GHRH analogue with N-terminal trans-3-hexenoyl group for pharmacokinetics. Reduces visceral adiposity.
biohacker_use: 1-2mg subcutaneous daily. Studied in HIV-associated lipodystrophy; used off-label for visceral fat reduction.

name: Selank
mechanism_brief: Synthetic tuftsin analogue with C-terminal Pro-Gly-Pro. Modulates BDNF, anxiolytic profile in rodent and small clinical studies.
biohacker_use: 300mcg intranasal, 1-3x/day. Used for anxiolysis, mild nootropic effect.

name: Semax
mechanism_brief: ACTH(4-10) analogue without endocrine activity; nootropic and neuroprotective via BDNF/NGF modulation.
biohacker_use: 300-600mcg intranasal daily for cognitive performance, post-stroke recovery research interest.

name: DSIP
mechanism_brief: Nonapeptide originally isolated from rabbit brain; promotes delta-wave EEG activity, may modulate ACTH and stress response.
biohacker_use: 100-300mcg subcutaneous before sleep. Used for sleep quality and stress modulation.

name: Epitalon
mechanism_brief: Khavinson tetrapeptide; reported telomerase activation and pineal gland modulation in cell and animal models.
biohacker_use: 5-10mg subcutaneous courses (10-20 day cycles). Used in longevity-focused stacks.

name: FOXO4-DRI

canonical_targets.name: Cellular tumor antigen p53
canonical_targets.mechanism_role: primary — disrupts FOXO4-p53 interaction in senescent cells

mechanism_brief: D-retro-inverso peptide that disrupts FOXO4-p53 interaction, selectively inducing apoptosis in senescent cells (senolytic).
biohacker_use: Investigational senolytic. Limited human dosing data; cycled administration discussed in research literature.

name: SS-31
mechanism_brief: Mitochondria-targeted tetrapeptide that binds cardiolipin to stabilise the inner membrane and improve respiratory efficiency.
biohacker_use: Investigational. Subcutaneous dosing studied in mitochondrial disease and primary mitochondrial myopathy trials.
